purpose: Markup and writing ontology for turning systematic readings into website cards, pages, timelines, maps, and scholarly syntheses.

artifact_types.label: Source Packet
artifact_types.description: Smallest verified source unit: document id, page range, extracted passage summary, key names, and claim candidates.

artifact_types.label: Section Summary
artifact_types.description: Exhaustive summary of a section/chapter/page range with claims, concepts, persons, and evidence anchors.

artifact_types.label: Claim Record
artifact_types.description: Atomic historical, textual, bibliographic, or interpretive assertion with evidence and review status.

artifact_types.label: Scholar Profile
artifact_types.description: Biography, corpus works, arguments, historiographical position, contribution to Pico/Renaissance/magic studies.

artifact_types.label: Pico Work Dossier
artifact_types.description: Aggregated notes on a Pico primary text, including structure, themes, debates, editions, and open problems.

artifact_types.label: Concept Dossier
artifact_types.description: Concept history and usage across Pico and scholarship, with debate map and related terms.

artifact_types.label: Historiography Node
artifact_types.description: Named debate or interpretive position with scholars, claims, tensions, and revisions.

artifact_types.label: Timeline Event
artifact_types.description: Life, publication, controversy, travel, reception, or scholarship event with date/place/source note.

artifact_types.label: Location Record
artifact_types.description: Geocoded place tied to Pico's life, education, travel, trouble, publication, or reception.

artifact_types.label: Website Card
artifact_types.description: Short browsable card for the public site.

artifact_types.label: Website Page
artifact_types.description: Long-form reviewed essay/page for the public site.

promotion_rules.source_packet: May produce claims and section summaries only after page anchors are recorded.
promotion_rules.section_summary: May promote to website page when every claim has a source page and confidence.
promotion_rules.scholar_profile: May promote when biography, corpus works, arguments, and historiographical position are separated.
promotion_rules.pico_work_dossier: May promote when primary text structure, themes, editions, scholarship, and open problems are recorded.
promotion_rules.timeline_event: May show publicly as DRAFT if evidence_status is likely or verified; provisional route events stay visually marked.
promotion_rules.location_record: May show on map if coordinates are known; uncertain historical role must be labeled.
